summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #743 where Attio's draft-07 JSON schemas caused OpenAI-compat tool schema normalization to fail, leaving nested anyOf structures that OpenAI rejects.
summary.outcome: Implemented a two-part fix: strip $schema before canonicalization so draft-07 schemas get AST processing, and add a SimplifyCompositeTransform to remove empty {} schemas left by not canonicalization. All 128 tests pass.

repo: SchemaDocument::from_json() rejects any $schema URI that isn't Draft 2020-12, causing the entire canonicalization path to be skipped for draft-07 schemas.
The canonicalization fallback path returned schema.clone() (preserving $schema) rather than the already-mutated input — a subtle bug in the error paths.
OpenAiSchemaSubsetTransform strips $schema anyway, so removing it earlier is semantically equivalent and safe.
schemars transforms canonicalize `not` to an empty schema {} (JSON Schema 'true'), which is valid per spec but rejected by OpenAI — no existing transform cleaned this up.
Test discovery requires --lib flag for unit tests in this crate; tests are under openai_compat::tests namespace.

code.finding: canonicalize_schema_for_openai_compat() — SchemaDocument::from_json() call that rejects non-2020-12 $schema URIs, root cause of the bug.
Fix location: $schema stripped from input before SchemaDocument::from_json() so draft-07 schemas get full canonicalization.
New SimplifyCompositeTransform: removes empty {} variants from anyOf/oneOf/allOf and collapses single-variant composites inline. Runs after OpenAiSchemaSubsetTransform.
New tests: sanitize_draft07_schema_strips_unsupported_keywords and responses_tools_draft07_attio_schema_normalized reproduce the exact Attio schema pattern from issue #743.

workflow: Test-first approach: add failing test to reproduce the bug before applying the fix, then confirm fix makes tests pass.
Initial fix (stripping $schema) was necessary but not sufficient — the empty {} artifact from not canonicalization required a second transform.
Running the broader crate test suite (128 tests) after changes is the right regression check for schema normalization work.

friction: Test discovery was non-obvious — needed --lib flag to find unit tests; initial test run attempts used wrong flags.
First version of the fix only stripped $schema but didn't handle the empty {} schemas that canonicalization of `not` produces, requiring a second iteration.
Initial test assertions were too weak (only checked keyword removal) and had to be strengthened to actually reproduce the bug.
open_items: SimplifyCompositeTransform removes empty {} (true schema) from anyOf variants, but this is a semantic change — empty {} in anyOf means 'accept anything', so removing it changes schema semantics. Worth a follow-up audit for edge cases.
No tests for draft-04 schemas, which would also be rejected by SchemaDocument::from_json() and now benefit from the $schema stripping fix.
The fallback warning log path still exists — worth monitoring in production to see if any schemas still fail canonicalization after the $schema strip.
